Sunday, June 21, 2026 Daily Digest

WBD Goes All-In on Agentic Ad-Tech, BCG Maps the CMO Gap, The B2B Buyer Paradox Deepens

Today's top stories in AI + enterprise tech

1

Warner Bros. Discovery Rebuilds Ad Stack Around Agentic AI

WBD is upgrading its entire ad-tech infrastructure with AWS-backed agentic AI — agents for media planning, dynamic forecasting, real-time campaign optimization, and closed-loop measurement. The goal: unify linear TV and digital buys under one AI-powered platform. Arriving just before Cannes Lions, this signals how seriously major publishers are taking the agentic shift.

Read on Marketing Dive →
2

BCG: 91% of B2C CMOs Say AI Is Reshaping Their Funnels

BCG's latest research shows the agentic marketing transformation is no longer theoretical. 91% of B2C CMOs (and 76% of B2B) say AI-moderated, no-click discovery is actively reshaping their already nonlinear funnels. One beauty company CMO captured the mood: "I'm racing to avoid a future where agent-native startups use these new tools to take share."

Read on BCG →
3

Yext Opens Full Agentic Marketing Platform to Enterprise

Yext is making its entire platform — verified brand data, Scout competitive intelligence, and agentic execution — accessible via MCP, API, and native apps. The pitch: "Agents without competitive intelligence automate mediocrity." They're tracking 10 billion signals across 12 million locations to give AI agents the local context they need.

Read on MarTech Series →
4

The AI Visibility Gap: Why B2B Brands Are Disappearing

AI-powered search is fundamentally changing how buyers discover vendors — and many B2B brands are invisible to it. The playbooks that worked for traditional search don't translate. For enterprise marketers, this means rethinking how you structure content, what claims you make, and how you position against competitors in AI-mediated discovery.

Read on ITMunch →
5

The 2026 B2B Buyer Paradox: More AI, More Paralysis

Here's an unexpected twist: buyers want self-directed, rep-free discovery — but the flood of AI-generated content is creating unprecedented analysis paralysis. Sales cycles are stalling despite faster research capabilities. The insight: differentiation matters more than ever when everything sounds the same.

Read on Salesfully →

💡 My Take

Read this one: The BCG piece on agentic marketing transformation. The stat that 91% of B2C CMOs see AI reshaping their funnels isn't marketing hype — it's coming from the CMOs themselves. The gap between "experimenting with AI" and "racing to avoid disruption" is closing fast. If you're still in pilot mode, you're already behind the leaders.
